en: A MASKIL OF ASAPH. Give ear, O my people, to my teaching; incline your ears to the words of my mouth!
zh: 我的民哪！你們要留心聽我的教訓， 側耳聽我口中的言語。

en: I will open my mouth in a parable; I will utter dark sayings from of old,
zh: 我要開口用比喻， 把古時隱祕的事說出來，

en: things that we have heard and known, that our fathers have told us.
zh: 就是我們所聽過所知道的， 也是我們的列祖告訴我們的，

en: We will not hide them from their children, but tell to the coming generation the glorious deeds of the LORD, and his might, and the wonders that he has done.
zh: 我們不把這些事向他們的子孫隱瞞， 卻要把耶和華應得的讚美、他的能力， 和他所行的奇事， 都向後代的人述說。

en: He established a testimony in Jacob and appointed a law in Israel, which he commanded our fathers to teach to their children,
zh: 他在雅各設立法度， 在以色列中制訂律法， 就是他吩咐我們的列祖， 去教訓他們的子孫的，

en: that the next generation might know them, the children yet unborn, and arise and tell them to their children,
zh: 好使後代的人，包括將要出生的兒女，都可以知道； 他們也要起來，告訴他們的子孫，

en: so that they should set their hope in God and not forget the works of God, but keep his commandments;
zh: 使他們信靠 神， 不忘記 神的作為， 謹守他的誡命；

en: and that they should not be like their fathers, a stubborn and rebellious generation, a generation whose heart was not steadfast, whose spirit was not faithful to God.
zh: 免得他們像他們的列祖， 成了頑梗悖逆的一代， 心不堅定的一代， 他們的心對 神不忠。

en: The Ephraimites, armed with the bow, turned back on the day of battle.
zh: 以法蓮的子孫，雖然備有弓箭， 在爭戰的日子，卻轉身逃走。

en: They did not keep God's covenant, but refused to walk according to his law.
zh: 他們不謹守 神的約， 不肯遵行他的律法。

en: They forgot his works and the wonders that he had shown them.
zh: 他們忘記了 神的作為， 和他顯給他們看的奇事。

en: In the sight of their fathers he performed wonders in the land of Egypt, in the fields of Zoan.
zh: 他在埃及地，在瑣安的田野， 在他們列祖的眼前，行了奇事。

en: He divided the sea and let them pass through it, and made the waters stand like a heap.
zh: 他把海分開，領他們走過去， 又使海水直立像一道堤壩。

en: In the daytime he led them with a cloud, and all the night with a fiery light.
zh: 白日他用雲彩， 夜間他用火光引導他們。

en: He split rocks in the wilderness and gave them drink abundantly as from the deep.
zh: 他在曠野使磐石裂開， 給他們水喝，多如深淵的水。

en: He made streams come out of the rock and caused waters to flow down like rivers.
zh: 他使水從磐石中湧出來， 使水好像江河一般流下。

en: Yet they sinned still more against him, rebelling against the Most High in the desert.
zh: 但他們仍然犯罪頂撞他， 在乾旱之地仍然悖逆至高者。

en: They tested God in their heart by demanding the food they craved.
zh: 他們心裡試探 神， 隨著自己的慾望要求食物。

en: They spoke against God, saying, "Can God spread a table in the wilderness?
zh: 他們妄論 神，說： “ 神可以在曠野擺設筵席嗎？

en: He struck the rock so that water gushed out and streams overflowed. Can he also give bread or provide meat for his people?"
zh: 他雖曾擊打磐石，使水湧出來， 好像江河氾濫， 他還能賜糧食嗎？ 他還能為自己的子民預備肉食嗎？”

en: Therefore, when the LORD heard, he was full of wrath; a fire was kindled against Jacob; his anger rose against Israel,
zh: 因此耶和華聽見了，就大怒； 有烈火在雅各燒起， 有怒氣向以色列發作。

en: because they did not believe in God and did not trust his saving power.
zh: 因為他們不相信 神， 不倚靠他的拯救。

en: Yet he commanded the skies above and opened the doors of heaven,
zh: 然而，他吩咐天上的雲彩， 打開天上的門戶。

en: and he rained down on them manna to eat and gave them the grain of heaven.
zh: 他降嗎哪給他們吃， 把天上的食物賜給他們。

en: Man ate of the bread of the angels; he sent them food in abundance.
zh: 於是人吃了天使的食物， 是 神賜下的食糧，使他們飽足。

en: He caused the east wind to blow in the heavens, and by his power he led out the south wind;
zh: 他從天空颳起東風， 施能力領出南風。

en: he rained meat on them like dust, winged birds like the sand of the seas;
zh: 他降肉食給他們，多如塵土； 又降飛鳥給他們，多如海沙。

en: he let them fall in the midst of their camp, all around their dwellings.
zh: 他使飛鳥落在他們的營中， 在他們住處的四圍。

en: And they ate and were well filled, for he gave them what they craved.
zh: 他們吃了，而且吃得很飽， 這樣， 神把他們所願的都賜給他們了。

en: But before they had satisfied their craving, while the food was still in their mouths,
zh: 他們還沒有因所願的滿足， 食物仍在他們口中的時候，

en: the anger of God rose against them, and he killed the strongest of them and laid low the young men of Israel.
zh: 神的怒氣就向他們發作， 殺了他們中間最肥壯的人， 擊倒了以色列的年輕人。

en: In spite of all this, they still sinned; despite his wonders, they did not believe.
zh: 雖然經歷了這一切，他們仍然犯罪； 儘管看見這些奇事，他們仍不相信。

en: So he made their days vanish like a breath, and their years in terror.
zh: 所以 神使他們的日子在虛空中消逝， 使他們的歲月在驚恐中完結。

en: When he killed them, they sought him; they repented and sought God earnestly.
zh: 神擊殺他們的時候，他們就尋求他； 他們回轉過來，切切求問 神。

en: They remembered that God was their rock, the Most High God their redeemer.
zh: 他們也想起 神是他們的磐石， 至高的 神是他們的救贖主。

en: But they flattered him with their mouths; they lied to him with their tongues.
zh: 但他們仍然用口欺騙他， 用舌頭向他說謊。

en: Their heart was not steadfast toward him; they were not faithful to his covenant.
zh: 他們的心對他不堅定， 也不忠於和他所立的約。

en: Yet he, being compassionate, atoned for their iniquity and did not destroy them; he restrained his anger often and did not stir up all his wrath.
zh: 他卻有憐憫，赦免罪孽，沒有把他們滅絕， 並且多次抑制自己的怒氣， 沒有完全宣洩他的烈怒。

en: He remembered that they were but flesh, a wind that passes and comes not again.
zh: 他顧念他們不過是人， 是一陣吹去不再返回的風。

en: How often they rebelled against him in the wilderness and grieved him in the desert!
zh: 他們多少次在曠野悖逆他， 在沙漠使他擔憂；

en: They tested God again and again and provoked the Holy One of Israel.
zh: 他們再三試探 神， 使以色列的聖者憂傷。

en: They did not remember his power or the day when he redeemed them from the foe,
zh: 他們忘記了他的能力 ， 就是他救贖他們脫離敵人的日子，

en: when he performed his signs in Egypt and his marvels in the fields of Zoan.
zh: 他怎樣在埃及顯神蹟， 在瑣安的田野行奇事。

en: He turned their rivers to blood, so that they could not drink of their streams.
zh: 他把他們的江河都變為血， 使他們不能喝河流的水。

en: He sent among them swarms of flies, which devoured them, and frogs, which destroyed them.
zh: 他使成群的蒼蠅到他們中間來，吞吃他們； 又使青蛙來毀滅他們。

en: He gave their crops to the destroying locust and the fruit of their labor to the locust.
zh: 他把他們的農產交給蚱蜢， 把他們辛勞的收穫交給蝗蟲。

en: He destroyed their vines with hail and their sycamores with frost.
zh: 他用冰雹摧毀他們的葡萄樹， 用嚴霜凍壞他們的桑樹。

en: He gave over their cattle to the hail and their flocks to thunderbolts.
zh: 又把他們的牲畜交給冰雹， 把他們的群畜交給閃電。

en: He let loose on them his burning anger, wrath, indignation, and distress, a company of destroying angels.
zh: 他使猛烈的怒氣、忿怒、憤恨、患難， 好像一群降災的使者，臨到他們中間。

en: He made a path for his anger; he did not spare them from death, but gave their lives over to the plague.
zh: 他為自己的怒氣修平了路， 不惜使他們死亡， 把他們的性命交給瘟疫。

en: He struck down every firstborn in Egypt, the firstfruits of their strength in the tents of Ham.
zh: 他在埃及擊殺了所有的長子， 在含的帳棚中擊殺了他們強壯時生的頭胎子。

en: Then he led out his people like sheep and guided them in the wilderness like a flock.
zh: 他卻把自己的子民領出來好像領羊群， 在曠野引導他們像引導群畜一樣。

en: He led them in safety, so that they were not afraid, but the sea overwhelmed their enemies.
zh: 他帶領他們平平安安地走過去，所以他們不懼怕； 海卻淹沒了他們的仇敵。

en: And he brought them to his holy land, to the mountain which his right hand had won.
zh: 他領他們進入自己聖地的境界， 到他右手所得的這山地。

en: He drove out nations before them; he apportioned them for a possession and settled the tribes of Israel in their tents.
zh: 他在他們面前把外族人趕出去， 用繩子量地，分給他們作為產業， 又使以色列眾支派的人居住在自己的帳棚裡。

en: Yet they tested and rebelled against the Most High God and did not keep his testimonies,
zh: 但他們仍然試探和悖逆至高的 神， 不謹守他的法則。

en: but turned away and acted treacherously like their fathers; they twisted like a deceitful bow.
zh: 他們背信不忠，像他們的列祖一樣； 他們改變了，如同不可靠的弓。

en: For they provoked him to anger with their high places; they moved him to jealousy with their idols.
zh: 因他們的邱壇，惹起他的怒氣， 因他們雕刻的偶像，激起他的憤恨。

en: When God heard, he was full of wrath, and he utterly rejected Israel.
zh: 神聽見就大怒， 完全棄絕了以色列。

en: He forsook his dwelling at Shiloh, the tent where he dwelt among mankind,
zh: 他丟棄了在示羅的居所， 就是他在世人中間居住的帳棚。

en: and delivered his power to captivity, his glory to the hand of the foe.
zh: 他又把象徵他權能的約櫃交給人擄去， 把他的榮美交在敵人的手裡，

en: He gave his people over to the sword and vented his wrath on his heritage.
zh: 並且把自己的子民交給刀劍， 向自己的產業大發烈怒。

en: Fire devoured their young men, and their young women had no marriage song.
zh: 他們的青年被火吞滅， 他們的少女也聽不見結婚的喜歌。

en: Their priests fell by the sword, and their widows made no lamentation.
zh: 他們的祭司倒在刀下， 他們的寡婦卻不能哀哭。

en: Then the Lord awoke as from sleep, like a strong man shouting because of wine.
zh: 那時主好像從睡眠中醒過來， 如同勇士酒後清醒一樣。

en: And he put his adversaries to rout; he put them to everlasting shame.
zh: 他擊退了他的敵人， 使他們永遠蒙羞受辱。

en: He rejected the tent of Joseph; he did not choose the tribe of Ephraim,
zh: 他棄絕約瑟的帳棚， 不揀選以法蓮支派，

en: but he chose the tribe of Judah, Mount Zion, which he loves.
zh: 卻揀選了猶大支派， 他所愛的錫安山。

en: He built his sanctuary like the high heavens, like the earth, which he has founded forever.
zh: 他建造了自己的聖所好像在高天之上， 又像他所建立永存的大地。

en: He chose David his servant and took him from the sheepfolds;
zh: 他揀選了自己的僕人大衛， 把他從羊圈中召出來；

en: from following the nursing ewes he brought him to shepherd Jacob his people, Israel his inheritance.
zh: 他領他出來，使他不再跟著那些母羊， 卻要牧養他的子民雅各， 和他的產業以色列。

en: With upright heart he shepherded them and guided them with his skillful hand.
zh: 於是大衛以正直的心牧養他們， 靈巧地引導他們。
